name: Logical Access Controls
description: Systems are protected against unauthorized access through logical access security measures
requirements: All user-facing endpoints must require authentication
Authorization checks must be performed before granting access to resources
Session tokens must expire after a configurable timeout period
Failed login attempts must be logged and rate-limited
Multi-factor authentication required for administrative access

name: Credential Management
description: Credentials for protected information assets are created, managed, and protected appropriately
requirements: Secrets must never be hardcoded in source code
API keys and tokens must be stored in a secrets manager (e.g., Azure Key Vault)
Passwords must be hashed using bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2
Encryption keys must be rotated according to policy (minimum annually)
Connection strings must use environment variables, not config files

name: Access Restriction
description: Access to systems and data is restricted based on need and principle of least privilege
requirements: Role-based access control (RBAC) must be implemented
Service accounts must have minimum necessary permissions
Database access must be restricted to application service accounts
Administrative functions must require elevated privileges

name: External Threat Protection
description: Systems are protected against threats from external sources
requirements: All user input must be validated and sanitized
SQL queries must use parameterized statements
HTML output must be encoded to prevent XSS
CSRF tokens must be implemented for state-changing operations
File uploads must be validated for type and size

name: Data Transmission Security
description: Data is protected during transmission using encryption and secure protocols
requirements: All external communications must use TLS 1.2 or higher
HTTP endpoints must redirect to HTTPS
API calls to external services must use HTTPS
Certificates must be valid and not self-signed in production

name: Malicious Software Prevention
description: Systems are protected against malicious software
requirements: Dynamic code execution (eval, new Function) must be avoided
Dependencies must be scanned for known vulnerabilities
Child process execution must be avoided or strictly controlled
User-provided file paths must be validated against directory traversal

name: Security Monitoring
description: Systems are monitored for anomalies that indicate security events
requirements: Security-relevant events must be logged with timestamps
Logs must include actor identity, action, and resource
Error conditions must be logged with sufficient context
Log data must not contain sensitive information (PII, secrets)

name: Activity Monitoring
description: Security events are identified and logged for analysis
requirements: Authentication events (login, logout, failure) must be logged
Data access events must be recorded in audit trails
Administrative actions must be logged with full context
Logs must be retained per organizational retention policy

name: Change Management
description: Changes to infrastructure and software are authorized, designed, developed, and implemented in a controlled manner
requirements: All code changes must go through pull request review
Database schema changes must be versioned and reversible
Configuration changes must be tracked and auditable
Deployments must follow the organization's CI/CD pipeline
